food_subgroup: Traditional herbal and fruit-based cold drinks

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a water chestnut food entity but no specific entry for water chestnut beverage/drink. The water chestnut food entity is used as the closest parent.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers water chestnut as an ingredient; no specific beverage preparation code exists. Used as closest parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code found for water chestnut drink or water chestnut beverage preparation.

serving.natural_language_portion: One upsize serving of water chestnut drink as sold at Qi Ji Singapore is approximately 500 ml, equivalent to 500 g, representing a large takeaway cup.

nutrient_source.title: Water chestnut juice / water chestnut drink — estimated from USDA FoodData Central raw water chestnut (NDB 11688) and standard beverage dilution ratios
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for water chestnut drink exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are estimated by applying a standard juice/decoction dilution factor (approximately 1 part water chestnut solids to 4–5 parts water with added sugar) to USDA FoodData Central raw water chestnut nutrient data (FDC ID 170073). Sugar content reflects typical Singaporean hawker-style sweetened preparation. This is a medium-confidence estimate.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for water chestnut drink. GI of 54 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for water chestnut (Eleocharis dulcis), which reports a GI of approximately 54 (medium-low range). As the beverage is a diluted, sweetened preparation, the effective GI is modulated by dilution and added sugar. GL per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (54 × 30.5) / 100 ≈ 16.5, based on 500 g serving with 6.0 g carbohydrate per 100 g = 30 g carbohydrate per serving.

hypertension.guidance: Water chestnut drink contains approximately 5 mg sodium per 100 ml, making it a low-sodium beverage suitable for individuals managing hypertension, though the added sugar content should be monitored in the context of overall dietary sodium and caloric intake.

type2_diabetes.guidance: The sweetened preparation of water chestnut drink contributes approximately 5.5 g sugar per 100 ml and a glycemic load of approximately 16.5 per upsize serving; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size or request reduced-sugar versions to manage postprandial blood glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Water chestnut drink is essentially fat-free and low in sodium, presenting minimal direct cardiovascular risk from lipid or sodium load; however, the added sugar content contributes to total caloric intake and should be considered in the context of a heart-healthy diet.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Water Chestnut Drink (Upsize) is a traditional Singaporean cold beverage made from water chestnuts (Eleocharis dulcis), a crunchy aquatic vegetable widely used in Chinese and Southeast Asian cuisine. The drink is prepared by boiling or juicing water chestnuts with water and rock sugar or white sugar, then chilling the resulting liquid. The flavour is mildly sweet, slightly earthy, and refreshing, with a clean, light finish. It is commonly sold at Singaporean hawker centres, coffee shops (kopitiams), and traditional drink stalls, including the Qi Ji chain. The upsize format typically refers to a larger cup of approximately 500 ml. Nutritionally, the drink is low in calories (approximately 25 kcal per 100 ml), contains negligible fat and protein, and derives most of its energy from added sugar and the natural carbohydrates of water chestnut. It is naturally gluten-free, dairy-free, vegan, and halal. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 54 (low-medium range), but the glycemic load per upsize serving is moderate at approximately 16.5 due to the large volume. Clinically, it is considered a relatively benign beverage for most individuals but should be consumed in moderation by those managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome due to its added sugar content. The drink is culturally significant as part of the traditional Chinese herbal and cooling drink tradition (liang cha), believed in traditional Chinese medicine to have heat-clearing and diuretic properties.
common_user_queries: How many calories in water chestnut juice?

confidence_reason: No direct nutrient entry for water chestnut drink exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA raw water chestnut data with a standard dilution and sweetening adjustment. GI is estimated from the closest analogous food (raw water chestnut) in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium due to the estimation basis.
notes: Fat_g is naturally zero as water chestnut drink is a dilute aqueous beverage with no added fat. Saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, and cholesterol_mg are confirmed zero by the nature of the preparation. Protein_g is 0.2 g per 100 ml, reflecting trace protein from diluted water chestnut solids. Vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA source for this preparation. Vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA source for this beverage preparation. Vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA source. Vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA source. Vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA source. Vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA source; water chestnut is a plant-based ingredient and contains no B12. Folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA source for this beverage preparation. Vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA source; plant-based beverages do not naturally contain vitamin D. Vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA source. Phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA source for this beverage preparation. Magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA source. Zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA source. Selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA source. The upsize designation refers to the serving size (approximately 500 ml) as sold at Qi Ji - Funan, Singapore. The food_group is retained as Beverages per the input record. GI set to 54 based on analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database water chestnut entry; gi_category is low per convention for GI below 55.
